name: Taj Mahal
location: The Taj Mahal is located on the southern bank of the Yamuna River in Agra, India, approximately 230 kilometers south of New Delhi.
history:
📍 Taj Mahal 

🌍 Uttar Pradesh, India

Short History: The Taj Mahal was built by Mughal Emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal between 1632 and 1653. It took around 22 years to complete and required the labor of thousands of skilled workers.

⭐ Why Famous: The Taj Mahal is famous for its stunning architecture, intricate marble work, and perfect proportions, making it one of the Seven Wonders of the Modern World.

📜 Story: The story goes that Shah Jahan was deeply in love with Mumtaz Mahal, who died during the birth of their 14th child. He was so grief-stricken that he decided to build a monument that would surpass all others in beauty and grandeur.

📌 Exact Location: The Taj Mahal is located on the southern bank of the Yamuna River in Agra, India, approximately 230 kilometers south of New Delhi.

name: Tirumala
location: 25 km from Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh, India (Latitude: 13.7333° N, Longitude: 78.4239° E)
history:
📍 Tirumala
 🌍 Andhra Pradesh, India

The temple of Lord Venkateswara has a history dating back to the 10th century. Initially built by King Narasimha Deva I in 1000 AD, it was later rebuilt and expanded.

⭐ Why Famous: Ttirupati is one of the holiest pilgrimage sites for Hindus, attracting millions of devotees every year.

📜 Story: The story goes that Lord Venkateswara, an incarnation of Lord Vishnu, descended to earth at Tirumala to help his wife, Sita. He later ascended back to heaven and promised to return when the time was right.

📌 Exact Location: 25 km from Chittoor, Andhra Pradesh, India (Latitude: 13.7333° N, Longitude: 78.4239° E)

name: Swiss Alps 🏔️
location: The Swiss Alps stretch across 11 cantons in Switzerland, covering an area of approximately 60,000 square kilometers (23,100 sq mi). The highest peak is the Dufourspitze, with an elevation of 4,634 meters (15,203 ft), located in the Valais region.
history:
Swiss Alps 🏔️

* State/Country: Switzerland
* Short History: The Swiss Alps have been a mountain range for over 60 million years, formed by the movement of the European tectonic plate. Over time, they have been shaped by glaciers and weathering processes.
* Why Famous: The Swiss Alps are famous for their breathtaking natural beauty, with towering peaks, picturesque villages, and crystal-clear lakes. They also offer a wide range of outdoor activities such as skiing, hiking, and mountaineering.
* Story: The Swiss Alps have played an important role in the country's history and culture. In ancient times, they were home to various tribes and settlements, including the Celts and Romans. Later, during the Middle Ages, the Alps became a popular destination for pilgrims on the Way of St. James.
* Exact Location: The Swiss Alps stretch across 11 cantons in Switzerland, covering an area of approximately 60,000 square kilometers (23,100 sq mi). The highest peak is the Dufourspitze, with an elevation of 4,634 meters (15,203 ft), located in the Valais region.
